data.topic: Always-on Android selective speaker transcription app: best practices for Android foreground services with continuous audio recording, on-device speaker verification, cloud STT APIs, Bluetooth audio routing, and battery-efficient GPS tracking

claims.claim: Microphone foreground service type is EXEMPT from Android 15's 6-hour timeout and audio-related wake locks are exempt from Android Vitals 2-hour penalty — making it the correct service type for always-on recording.

Microphone FGS CANNOT be started from background, BOOT_COMPLETED, or via CompanionDeviceManager — RECORD_AUDIO is a while-in-use permission. The ONLY safe start path is from a foreground Activity with user interaction.

Doze mode ignores PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK for all apps UNLESS on battery optimization whitelist. Apps must request ACTION_REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS (user consent required, Play Store policy restricted) to retain wake locks and network during Doze.

START_STICKY restarts on Android 12+ are treated as background starts — may throw ForegroundServiceStartNotAllowedException if app is background at restart time. The only durable pattern is to never let the service die.

Samsung One UI 7 predictive engine pre-kills services after 4+ hours of predicted inactivity. Xiaomi HyperOS 2 requires proprietary 'Background Autostart' permission. App MUST include OEM-specific onboarding UX guiding users through 'Never sleeping apps' / autostart settings.

WorkManager heartbeat watchdog (15-min PeriodicWorkRequest) can restart killed services, but on Android 12+ must use setExpedited() and is subject to job quotas. Safety net, not primary reliability mechanism.

Google Play requires formal declaration for microphone FGS including feature description, user impact statement, and video demonstration. Wake lock enforcement began March 1, 2026: >2hr non-exempt wake locks in >5% sessions triggers warnings.

Android 14+ requires foregroundServiceType in manifest, FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MICROPHONE permission, RECORD_AUDIO runtime permission, and explicit type constant passed to startForeground(). Missing any causes crash.

synthesized_findings.key_insight: Microphone FGS is the golden path (timeout-exempt, Vitals-exempt) but has a hard constraint: must be user-initiated from foreground. Architecture must ensure the service never dies once started, backed by battery optimization whitelist + OEM-specific onboarding + WorkManager watchdog.

claims.claim: sherpa-onnx provides production-ready on-device speaker verification for Android with ECAPA-TDNN and 3D-Speaker (ERes2Net) models in ONNX format. Includes speaker_embedding_extractor, speaker_embedding_manager, and cosine_similarity utilities.
ECAPA-TDNN (SpeechBrain) produces 192-dimensional speaker embeddings. The model can be exported to ONNX format for mobile inference. VoxCeleb1 test EER is approximately 0.80%.
Silero VAD is a lightweight voice activity detection model available for Android/Kotlin, operating at 16kHz with low latency. Can gate speaker verification to only run during detected speech.

synthesized_findings.key_insight: sherpa-onnx is the most promising path for on-device speaker verification on Android — provides ECAPA-TDNN and 3D-Speaker models with ONNX Runtime inference. Silero VAD for speech gating. PARTIAL RESEARCH: Gemini agents failed, specific TFLite conversion details, inference latency benchmarks, and minimum enrollment duration data are gaps.

claims.claim: AssemblyAI Universal-3 Pro leads on noisy audio WER at 9.97% (vs Deepgram Nova-3 at 14.12%, OpenAI Whisper at 11.63%) on real-world noisy benchmarks, at $3.50/1,000 minutes.

Google Chirp 3 achieves lowest overall WER (~3.1-4.6% on AA-WER) with built-in denoiser, but costs $16.00/1,000 min (4-6x competitors). Dynamic batch at $4.00/1,000 min but 24hr turnaround and 20-min file limit with word timestamps.

Deepgram Nova-3 at $7.70/1,000 min supports 100+ audio formats, word-level timestamps at millisecond precision, and keyterm prompting (100 domain terms). Nova-3 specifically designed for noisy, far-field, crosstalk audio.
OpenAI Whisper-1 is batch-only (streaming ignored). gpt-4o-transcribe supports both batch and streaming at $6.00/1,000 min. Whisper degrades dramatically in heavy noise (15-29% WER).

Deepgram explicitly recommends NOT applying noise preprocessing before STT — spectral subtraction improves SNR by 8dB but drives WER UP 15%. Only VAD trimming is endorsed (30-40% cost savings by removing silence).
Word-level timestamps are included at no additional cost for both Deepgram (Nova-2/3) and AssemblyAI (Universal-2/3 Pro).
AssemblyAI Slam-1 is a speech-language model supporting prompt-based customization with up to 1,000 domain terms at $2.50/1,000 min, achieving 4.1% WER on AA-WER benchmark.

synthesized_findings.key_insight: AssemblyAI Universal-3 Pro is the best fit for noisy field audio: lowest noisy WER (9.97%), competitive pricing ($3.50/1K min), word-level timestamps included, and promptable context for domain terminology. Do NOT preprocess audio for noise — send raw with VAD trimming only.

claims.claim: startBluetoothSco() deprecated at API 31 (not 33). Replacement: setCommunicationDevice(AudioDeviceInfo) with getAvailableCommunicationDevices() and clearCommunicationDevice().
setCommunicationDevice() returns boolean = 'accepted' not 'complete'. SCO hardware handshake takes 500-1500ms after return. Must add 500-1000ms post-callback delay and discard initial audio frames.
HFP/SCO caps at 8kHz (CVSD narrowband) or 16kHz (mSBC wideband, HFP 1.6+). LE Audio with LC3 supports 32-48kHz bidirectional. Android 14+ adds HFP 1.9 Super Wideband at 32kHz (LC3-SWB).

LE Audio recording targets 32kHz sample rate with ENCODING_PCM_16BIT via AudioDeviceInfo.TYPE_BLE_HEADSET (API 33+). Must use setCommunicationDevice() — startBluetoothSco() will NOT detect LE Audio devices.

On Bluetooth disconnect, AudioRecord MUST be stopped and recreated (not just rerouted) because SCO sample rate (8-16kHz) differs from built-in mic native rate. Failing to recreate causes distorted audio.
setCommunicationDevice() requires MODE_IN_COMMUNICATION to be set BEFORE the routing request. MODE_NORMAL causes silent ignore or immediate revert.

Samsung S23-S26 exhibit 200-500ms phantom delay after 'Connected' state before audio path opens. Pixel 7-10 VOICE_COMMUNICATION source gets 'stolen' by Hey Google, producing silent PCM. Defense: stabilization delay + discard initial frames + fallback to VOICE_RECOGNITION source.
AudioDeviceCallback (API 23+) fires onAudioDevicesAdded/Removed for BT events. TYPE_BLUETOOTH_SCO for classic, TYPE_BLE_HEADSET for LE Audio. Does NOT require BLUETOOTH_CONNECT permission on API 31+.
NoiseSuppressor.isAvailable() is unreliable — many mid-range devices report true but produce silent recordings. VOICE_RECOGNITION audio source bypasses aggressive OEM noise cancellation.

synthesized_findings.key_insight: Bluetooth routing is the most fragile component. Must implement: (1) setCommunicationDevice() with MODE_IN_COMMUNICATION pre-set, (2) 500-1000ms stabilization delay after callback, (3) discard first ~500ms of audio, (4) recreate AudioRecord on disconnect (sample rate changes), (5) fallback from VOICE_COMMUNICATION to VOICE_RECOGNITION if silence detected. LE Audio (32kHz) is far better for speaker verification than SCO (8-16kHz).

claims.claim: PRIORITY_BALANCED_POWER_ACCURACY provides ~100m accuracy (city block level), NOT ~10m as hypothesized. Uses WiFi/cell only. PRIORITY_HIGH_ACCURACY with GPS required for 10m accuracy at significantly higher battery cost.
setMaxUpdateDelayMillis() enables location batching: setting 2x+ the interval allows batch delivery, reducing device wake-ups. Example: compute every 10 min, deliver every 60 min.

Android 14+ requires foreground service to declare both types in manifest (location|microphone) and both permissions (FOREGROUND_SERVICE_LOCATION, FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MICROPHONE). startForeground() must bitwise-OR both type constants.

RNNoise model is ~85KB (8-bit quantized), runs 60x faster than real-time on x86, ~7x on Raspberry Pi 3, with <10ms latency. But operates at 48kHz natively — 16kHz audio requires resample-up/down (adding overhead).

Applying noise reduction before ASR can DEGRADE accuracy — modern STT models are trained on noisy data. Only beneficial in very low SNR scenarios. Android's built-in NoiseSuppressor is NOT recommended for VOICE_RECOGNITION source.
GPS timestamp synchronization with audio requires better than 5-second accuracy. SystemClock.elapsedRealtimeNanos() is recommended for synchronizing sensor events with audio timestamps.

synthesized_findings.key_insight: Skip audio noise preprocessing entirely — it hurts STT accuracy and is unnecessary with modern models. For GPS, PRIORITY_HIGH_ACCURACY is needed for 10m accuracy (BALANCED only gives 100m), but use batching (setMaxUpdateDelayMillis) to reduce battery impact. Combine service types via bitwise-OR in a single foreground service.

key_insights: Microphone FGS is timeout-exempt and Vitals wake-lock-exempt — the golden path
Must be user-initiated from foreground (no background/boot start possible)
Battery optimization whitelist essential for Doze survival
OEM killing (Samsung/Xiaomi) requires onboarding UX, not code
sherpa-onnx provides production-ready ECAPA-TDNN for Android
Silero VAD for speech gating before verification
PARTIAL: inference latency, model size, enrollment duration are gaps
AssemblyAI Universal-3 Pro leads noisy WER at best price
Do NOT preprocess audio — send raw, VAD trim only
Word-level timestamps included free with top providers
SCO caps at 8-16kHz; LE Audio gives 32-48kHz for better verification
Must recreate AudioRecord on BT disconnect (sample rate change)
Multiple OEM-specific silent audio bugs require defense-in-depth
BALANCED accuracy is 100m, not 10m — need HIGH_ACCURACY for field use
Location batching reduces battery drain significantly
Skip noise preprocessing — hurts modern STT more than helps

conflicts.credibility_assessment: Gemini's source (direct API reference) is more authoritative. API 31 is the correct deprecation level — setCommunicationDevice() was introduced in API 31 as the replacement.

conflicts.topic: Noise preprocessing impact on STT accuracy

gemini_claim.claim: Noise preprocessing drives WER up 15% according to Deepgram

claude_claim.claim: Noise suppression is still beneficial in very low SNR scenarios to prevent mis-triggering

conflicts.credibility_assessment: Both claims are from Deepgram. Not a true conflict — noise preprocessing hurts in normal conditions but helps in extreme low-SNR. For field environments with variable noise, skip preprocessing and let the STT model handle it; only consider preprocessing if SNR is consistently below ~5dB.

gaps: Speaker verification: ECAPA-TDNN inference latency on Android ARM processors (Snapdragon/MediaTek) — no benchmarks found
Speaker verification: minimum enrollment duration for reliable verification (10s? 30s? 60s?) — no authoritative source
Speaker verification: TFLite vs ONNX Runtime performance comparison on Android for speaker embeddings
Speaker verification: impact of 8-16kHz Bluetooth SCO audio on speaker verification EER — no biometric-specific data
Cloud STT: no independent benchmarks for industrial field audio (construction, machinery) — all benchmarks use general noisy datasets
Cloud STT: batch processing turnaround latency comparison across providers not documented
GPS: no quantitative battery consumption data (mAh) comparing HIGH_ACCURACY vs BALANCED — only qualitative
GPS: synchronization precision between FusedLocationProvider timestamps and AudioRecord buffer timestamps
Foreground service: exact behavior when OEM kills microphone FGS — does audio hardware pipeline survive independently of wake locks?
Foreground service: real-world battery drain for 8+ hours continuous microphone recording — no published benchmarks
